title: The Queer Internet: LGBTQ Digital Discourse
further_info: Recommended Reading Hall, K. (1996). Cyberfeminism. In Computer-mediated communication: Linguistic, social, and cross-cultural perspectives (pp. 147-172). John Benjamins publishing company. Ilbury, C. (2020). “Sassy Queens”: Stylistic orthographic variation in Twitter and the enregisterment of AAVE. Journal of sociolinguistics, 24(2), 245-264. Jones, L. (2025). Language and LGBTQ+ youth: Analysing marginalised identities through an intersectional lens. Bloomsbury Publishing. Kibbey, T. E. (Ed.). (2023). Linguistics out of the closet: The interdisciplinarity of gender and sexuality in language science (Vol. 3). Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG. King, B. W., & Crowley, A. (2023). The future of pronouns in the online/offline nexus. In The Routledge Handbook of Pronouns (pp. 74-86). Routledge. Zottola, A. (2024). LGBTQ+ and feminist digital activism: A linguistic perspective. Cambridge University Press.
